7. Selfie Follow up Video Texts Get an iphone.mp4 Okay, so a fun story real quick. This particular tip I'm about to give you, I actually had a— he's the head of the sales team in a client deal. This guy taught people how to start. Airbnb businesses. It was a $15,000 information product. And the head of this particular sales team, he was already working on the deal before we started, which is, you know, common case. We come in and we typically give all kinds of tips, like what you're learning inside of this program. This particular tip, this guy literally laughed at me, he said, 'Oh, there's no way that we're going to be able to do that. No, that's not a scalable action. There's not a chance we're going to do that And fun fact, that guy got fired about three weeks later because the business had been constrained at about $500,000 a month in revenue for like two years. We came into the deal. saw very clearly we were like, 'Listen, this closer right here. They're giving us a ton of resistance to just basic ideas that don't take more than like 30 seconds. That are gonna make far more money off all the leads that are generated. Business owners want you to take actions like what I'm about to describe. They don't want you to make excuses about what I'm about to articulate to you.' And the lesson is very simple: selfie videos. First of all, yourself an iPhone. Androids come through like absolute shit when you send or receive a selfie video text. All we want to do is: we want to pull out our iPhone and, when we get a lead or when we're trying to follow up with a lead, as an example, we just want to shoot like a quick 10 to 30 second selfie video text. It looked like this as an example. Hey, what's going on, Leo? I saw that you filled out our form on Facebook. My name is Jeremy. I just wanted to put a face to the name. I got time in about five minutes if you're available to talk. If you can't talk now, I got time. Later tonight if you're available. You can also just shoot me over a text if you have a few questions. I just wanted to put a face to the name, you know, introduce myself and say that I'm very excited to help you out. All right, talk soon. Done. That's it. Less than 30 seconds. Send. On to the next thing. Don't make excuses about that tip. What it does is it substantially increases the connection rate. People don't like to let other people down. Think in your brain real quick. How easy is it to ignore an automated text? When you get those stupid reminder texts about some dinner reservation you got or a trainer appointment or even just a scheduled call that you booked, you don't even think about responding to them. A lot of people nowadays, they're not dumb. They know how the internet works. They know how automated texts work. They know how automated emails work. That's why nobody ever responds to texts or emails nowadays that look autonomous. Versus when I take out my phone. I take 10 to 30 seconds to send a very simple selfie video, I'm taking advantage of human psychology. There's a much higher probability that somebody's going to respond to me because I've initially established that I'm a real person actually trying to reach out to them. It's much harder to ignore a real person than it is to ignore an autonomous text. Do the selfie videos, watch your connection rate go through the roof, and don't make excuses about it. That's the worst thing that you could do with this specific advice.
